title: Aftershave balm
subtitle: A witch hazel and aloe vera balm with cedarwood and vetiver for post-shave skin
excerpt: Witch hazel reduces razor redness; aloe vera gel soothes and hydrates the freshly shaved skin; cedarwood and vetiver add a warm, masculine wood note. Absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy film.
sourceNotes: Witch hazel anti-inflammatory and astringent activity: Erdelmeier et al., Planta Medica (1996). Aloe vera soothing properties on irritated skin: Surjushe et al., Indian Journal of Dermatology (2008).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each bottle to 100 ml for correct concentration.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in small batches; the aloe vera gel limits shelf life to two to three months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within two months. Refrigerate to extend shelf life. Discard if the liquid separates into distinct layers that will not re-combine on shaking, or if it develops an unusual smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam-distilled extract of Hamamelis virginiana with 14% alcohol. The standard pharmacy aftershave base for hundreds of years -- astringent, anti-inflammatory, and mildly antiseptic.

content.text: as the post-shave base: it tightens pores, eases razor redness, and its mild alcohol keeps the product stable. Aloe vera gel adds hydration; vegetable glycerine stops it feeling tight; cedarwood and vetiver add a clean wood scent.

items.prepNote: about 0.2 ml; adds a light citrus top note

content.text: Combine the witch hazel, aloe vera gel, and vegetable glycerine in the bottle. Shake to mix -- the aloe gel will incorporate into the witch hazel. Add the essential oils, cap, and shake again. The product will have a slightly gel-like, cloudy appearance.

Shake before use. After shaving, rinse off any remaining shaving soap and pat the skin dry. Pour a small amount (about 5 ml, a teaspoon) into the palm and apply to the shaved area. The witch hazel will sting slightly on nicks or cuts -- this is normal. The sting subsides in 30 seconds. Do not apply to broken skin if sensitivity is a concern.

Keep at room temperature or in the refrigerator (the cool temperature feels soothing on application). Use within two months. Shake before each use. Discard if the liquid separates permanently or develops an off smell.
